headlines.title: Mortgage rates rise and deals pulled over Iran war turmoil
headlines.description: Average mortgage rates hit highest since last August in the biggest upheaval since the mini-Budget.

headlines.title: Pro-Palestinian march banned after government approves Met request
headlines.description: Organisers say it is a peaceful pro-Palestinian event but it has been criticised for representing the Iranian regime.

headlines.title: The Aldi-style disruptors who could be about to shake up the vets market
headlines.description: As pet owners complain of rising prices, independent practices want to take on the big chains.

headlines.title: First Mandelson files to be published on Wednesday
headlines.description: The documents are expected to detail parts of the process prior to Lord Mandelson's appointment as ambassador.

headlines.title: Jeffrey Epstein had two key aides - why do they still control his money and secrets?
headlines.description: Richard Kahn and Darren Indyke administer Epstein’s estate - court filings allege complicity in his crimes.

headlines.title: Gale-force winds to batter UK as snow and cold set for comeback
headlines.description: Strong winds are likely across many parts of the UK over the next few days before turning colder with some wintry showers at the end of the week, as Simon King explains.

headlines.title: Member of Iranian football delegation granted Australian asylum changes her mind
headlines.description: One of the seven women who received visas has reversed her decision after speaking to teammates, minister says

headlines.title: Hereditary peers to be removed from Lords as bill passes
headlines.description: The bill abolishes the 92 seats reserved for peers who inherit their titles through their families.

headlines.title: 'I am no spy': Courier in Russian exploding parcels plot against UK talks to BBC
headlines.description: Aleksandr Suranovas, charged with carrying out an act of terrorism for Russia, speaks to the BBC.

headlines.title: Secret of hedgehog hearing discovered by scientists
headlines.description: Researchers played a sountrack to hedgehogs to identify the frequency of sounds they can hear

headlines.title: UK ticket holder wins £181m EuroMillions jackpot
headlines.description: Tuesday's win is the third-largest in UK National Lottery history, operator Allwyn says.

headlines.title: Steve Rosenberg: Russia seeks diplomatic and economic gains from Iran war
headlines.description: President Putin pits himself as a potential mediator but that's not an easy sell, writes the BBC's Russia editor.

headlines.title: Strait of Hormuz: What happens if Iran shuts global oil corridor?
headlines.description: Iran says it will "set fire" to ships trying to sail through the world's most vital oil transit point.

headlines.title: Iran war cost will be passed to consumers, shipping giant boss tells BBC
headlines.description: Maersk chief executive Vincent Clerc has called on the US, Israel and Iran to reach "some kind of deal".

headlines.title: UK warship HMS Dragon departs for eastern Mediterranean
headlines.description: The Type 45 destroyer's main role will be protecting RAF Akrotiri, which was hit with an Iranian-made drone.

headlines.title: Trump will welcome Iran at World Cup, says Fifa president
headlines.description: US President Donald Trump has said Iran are "welcome" to take part in this summer's World Cup despite the countries being at war, according to Fifa president Gianni Infantino.

headlines.title: 'Icky and heartbreaking': The $2 per hour worker behind the OnlyFans boom
headlines.description: The BBC talks to a Philippines-based woman paid to pretend to be an OnlyFans star in online chats.

headlines.title: How Tottenham goalkeeper's horror night unfolded before 17th-minute substitution
headlines.description: Tottenham replace goalkeeper Antonin Kinsky in the 17th minute after conceding three goals inside the opening quarter of an hour of the Champions League last-16 tie at Atletico Madrid.

headlines.title: From New Zealand to Orkney: Why we brought this classic car half way round the world
headlines.description: Dutchman Hans Compter travelled around the world to deliver the Austin to Cathleen Hourie in Orkney.

headlines.title: Meeting my death-row pen pal after 20 years
headlines.description: Rhys Williams became friends with convicted killer Roderick Orme after the pair began exchanging letters.

headlines.title: The Papers:  'US bombers take off' and 'Heading out - at last'
headlines.description: US fighter jets leave UK airbase and British warship sails to Mediterranean -Wednesday’s papers.

headlines.title: Wildlife to replace historical figures on banknotes - and you get a say
headlines.description: The public will help choose which animals and birds will appear on the Bank of England's new notes.

headlines.title: Meghan to appear at £1,400 per person 'girls' weekend' in Australia
headlines.description: The Duke and Duchess of Sussex are returning to Australia in April, their first visit since 2018.

headlines.title: University tuition fees system is a 'mess', says Sir Nick Clegg
headlines.description: The ex-deputy PM told the BBC he would "take on the chin" any criticism surrounding his involvement

headlines.title: MPs demand urgent action on London grooming gangs
headlines.description: The government's inquiry into grooming gangs "must look specifically at London as part of its local investigations", says a group of MPs.

headlines.title: Some Glasgow Central trains resume - but station remains closed after fire
headlines.description: The station's low level platforms, which serve the Argyle line north of the River Clyde, have been reopened after a full site assessment

headlines.title: Glen Sannox ferry needs £3.2m repairs after one year in service
headlines.description: MSPs have been told the troubled CalMac vessel needs new propellors as part of multi-million pound repairs.

headlines.title: Woman charged with attempted murder after shooting at Rihanna's home
headlines.description: The woman posted about the singer online prior to the attack, the BBC's US news partner CBS reports.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Will War In Iran Really End "Very Soon"?
headlines.description: President Trump says Iran war will be over “very soon”.

headlines.title: Americanswers… on 5Live! Why is Trump’s White House comparing the war in Iran to a video game?
headlines.description: And how much is the war in Iran motivated by oil?

headlines.title: Is Tudor's Spurs reign almost up after only four games?
headlines.description: Igor Tudor's Spurs tenure could already be on the line after a disastrous defeat against Atletico Madrid in the Champions League, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

headlines.title: How Kinsky's horror night unfolded before 17th-minute substitution
headlines.description: Tottenham replace goalkeeper Antonin Kinsky in the 17th minute after conceding three goals inside the opening quarter of an hour of the Champions League last-16 tie at Atletico Madrid.

headlines.title: Sense of missed opportunity for Newcastle after late Barca blow
headlines.description: Eddie Howe's Newcastle were so close to a famous European victory, but now face a herculean second-leg task after Lamine Yamal's late penalty.

headlines.title: Trump will welcome Iran at World Cup - Infantino
headlines.description: US President Donald Trump has said Iran are "welcome" to take part in this summer's World Cup despite the countries being at war, according to Fifa president Gianni Infantino.

headlines.title: 'It's Wilt, me, then Kobe' - Adebayo scores 83 points in single game
headlines.description: The Miami Heat's Bam Adebayo scores 83 points in a game, surpassing Kobe Bryant and second only to the great Wilt Chamberlain.

headlines.title: Pressure of representing England has shown - Borthwick
headlines.description: England head coach Steve Borthwick says the shirt has "weighed heavy" on some of his players at times in a disappointing Six Nations campaign.
